briefs.title: Burnaby Approves New Zoning Bylaw Replacing 1965 Version
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a new zoning bylaw to replace the one from 1965. The new bylaw includes updated rules for development, such as new guidelines for development permit areas and changes to zoning districts. It also includes tenant protection measures and requirements for rental housing.
briefs.action_required: Review the new bylaw and development guidelines to understand how they affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL
AND REPLACEMENT
[image]
1.Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement
2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
2026-04-28_.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development
Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
4.Attachment 3 - Proposed Streamside Protection and
Enhancement Development Permit Area Guidelines
2026-04-28.pdf
5.Attachment 4 - Proposed Tenant Protection Development
Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
6.Attachment 5 - Proposed Zone Reassignment Reference Maps
2026-04-28.pdf
7.Attachment 6 - Rental Replacement and Inclusionary
Requirements Evaluation 2026-04-28.pdf
8.Attachment 7 - Example Inclusionary and Rental
Replacement at Occupancy and Second Tenancy
2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning
Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning
Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area
Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Seeks Funding Approval for Year 2 Operations
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has asked Burnaby City Council to approve funding for its second year of operations. This funding will help the BHA continue its work.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2
OPERATING FUNDING
[image]
1.BHA 2026 Operating Funding 2026-04-28_.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - BHA Assistance Request for Year 2 Operating
Funds 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing
Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating
Funding.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves 2026 Property Tax Rates for Municipal, Regional, and BIA
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the 2026 property tax rates. These rates cover municipal services, regional services, and Business Improvement Areas (BIAs, which are specific areas where businesses work together to improve their local business environment).
briefs.action_required: Review the 2026 property tax rates to understand your business's tax obligations.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
[image]
1.2026 Property Tax rates 2026-04-28__.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026_2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal,
regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax
rates.

briefs.title: Approving Officer Appointment: Council Approval Needed
briefs.summary: The council needs to approve the appointment of an Approving Officer. This role is important for decisions on land development and subdivisions.
briefs.action_required: No action required. This is for awareness.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
[image]
1.Appointment of Approving Officer 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving
Officer.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Variance for Retaining Wall Height at 8012 11th Avenue
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01. This permit allows a change to the rules about how high retaining walls can be at the property located at 8012 11th Avenue. The permit was sought to allow for retaining walls that don't meet the usual height rules.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING
WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
[image]
1.DVP26-01 - 8012 11th Avenue 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - DVP 26-01 - Sketch1 and 2 2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - DVP 26-01 - Permit 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance
Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Property and Liability Insurance Program Contract Renewals Requested for 2026
briefs.summary: Approval is being requested to award contracts for annual property and liability insurance programs for 2026. This ensures the continuation of insurance coverage for properties and potential liabilities.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT
RENEWALS

[image]
1.2026 Insurance Program - Contract Renewals -
2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for
the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Community Heritage Commission Members Seek Approval to Attend Heritage BC Conference
briefs.summary: The Community Heritage Commission (CHC) wants council approval for its members to attend the Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. This conference is an annual event.

key_dates.label: Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC
Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC

briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
[image]
1.2026 Heritage BC Conference 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the
Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026
Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6,
2026.

briefs.title: Networking Software Licenses Contract Award Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract for networking software licenses. This ensures the city has the necessary software for its network operations.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
[image]
1.CA - Networking Software Licenses 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: New Civic Facility: Council Approves Contract for Furniture
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract for supplying and installing office and facility furniture for the new civic facility. This ensures the building will be ready for use with the necessary furnishings.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS
AND EQUIPMENT
[image]
1.CA - New Civic Facility Fit-Out 2026-04-28 ___.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility
furniture.

briefs.title: Council approval sought for External Audit Services contract change.
briefs.summary: A contract change for external audit services needs Council approval. This ensures the city's financial records are properly reviewed by an outside firm. The contract covers the period up to April 28, 2026.

key_dates.label: External Audit Services contract end date

briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - External Audit Services 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (External Audit Services)
2026-04-28 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex: Council Approves Geotechnical Consulting Contract Change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a change to the contract for geotechnical (soil and rock) consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project. This ensures the project has the necessary expertise for ground-related issues.

Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake
Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Spring 2026 Community Grants: Application Approvals
briefs.summary: The city council is reviewing applications for the Spring 2026 Community Grant program. These grants support local initiatives in areas like recreation, food security, and community programs. Council approval is needed for the recommended grant recipients to receive funding.
briefs.action_required: Check the list of recommended grant recipients to see if your organization is included.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT
APPLICATIONS

3.Appendix B - Operating Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
4.Appendix C - Initiative Grants 2026.04.28.pdf

5.Appendix D - Subsidized Use Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
6.Appendix E - Food Security Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant
recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Fees and Charges Bylaw: Changes to Various Fees
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing some fees in its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. These changes affect fees listed in Schedule F (effective June 1, 2026) and Schedule F3 (effective September 1, 2026). The bylaw lists all municipal fees.
briefs.action_required: Review Schedules F and F3 to see if the fee changes affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Schedule F fee changes take effect
Burnaby Schedule F3 fee changes take effect

briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND
CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR
AMENDMENTS

Mid-Year Amendments 2026-04-28_.pdf

2026 2026-04-28.pdf

Septembr 1, 2026 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain
fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and
Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Introduce New Tenant Protection Bylaw
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is creating a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. This bylaw aims to protect renters during building redevelopments. It sets rules for how landlords must support tenants who are displaced due to redevelopment.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw to understand your responsibilities as a landlord.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION
FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW
AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
[image]
1.PDC - Proposed Tenant Protection Framework
2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw
2026 2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Key Terms and Changes between TAP and the
proposed Burnaby TPB 2026-04-28.pdf
4.Attachment 3 - Proposed Amendments to Burnaby Bylaw

5.Attachment 4 - List of in-progress Rezoning Applications
subject to TAP 2026-04-28.pdf

Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant
protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw
and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project: Council Approval Sought for Proposed Improvements
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is seeking council approval for improvements to the Southeast Bikeway. The project aims to upgrade the existing bikeway to improve safety and accessibility for cyclists and other users.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed changes to see how they may affect your business.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT -
PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
[image]
1.TC - Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project - Proposed
Improvements - 2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Project Scope - Southeast Bikeway
2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Southeast Area Proposed Improvements
(bikeway) 2026-04-28 .pdf
4.Attachment 3 - Big Bend Area Proposed Cross-Sections
(bikeway) 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed
improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade
Project.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Improve Pedestrian and Cyclist Safety at Intersections
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing its Town Centre Design Standards. The change adds "protected intersection geometry." This will improve safety for pedestrians and cyclists. It supports mobility for people of all ages and abilities.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY
TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO
INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION
GEOMETRY
[image]
1.TC - Proposed Amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre
Design Standards 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby
Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected
intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and
cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Exempting Some Properties from Density Requirements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is considering whether to exempt some properties in the Brentwood Park area from new provincial rules that allow for more density (more housing units in an area). This is because some of these properties have restrictive covenants (agreements that limit how the land can be used) or statutory building schemes (plans that control development). The council is discussing whether these existing agreements should override the new provincial density rules.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council decisions on Brentwood Park exemptions.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION -
RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY
BUILDING SCHEMES
[image]
1.Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and
Statutory Building Schemes 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion
regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with
Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning changes add hotel, restaurant, and housing units to Grand Promenade area.
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing the zoning for the area around 3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., and 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place. The changes allow for more hotel and restaurant space instead of office space. The changes also add 362 more housing units and increase the height of two towers. The developer will move the requirement to include affordable housing units to another site.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950
Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
[image]
1.14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand
Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf

Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received
Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two
market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73
(Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail
and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of
the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and
add 362 additional market strata units from the development
concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional
seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In
addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to provide the
inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from
the previous rezoning approval.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones Kingsway property for 12-storey mixed-use development
briefs.summary: Burnaby approved rezoning for 6535 Kingsway. The change allows a 12-storey building with 290 homes, commercial space at street level, and underground parking. The plan follows the City's official community plan and the Edmonds Community Plan.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
[image]
1.14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535
Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use
multiple-family development with a double-height commercial
storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units,
ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the
applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations
for the site.
(Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Zoning Change for New Self-Storage Building on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a zoning change to allow a five-story self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The decision follows the city's official community plan (OCP) policies from 1998 and transitional rules.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
[image]
1.14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland
Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed
self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in
accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and
approved transitionary provisions.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby E-Bike, E-Scooter Pilot Program: New Rules in Effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its street and traffic rules to include a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This means new rules are in place for where and how these devices can be used within the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw for e-bike and e-scooter rules.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image]
1.14811.Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961 Amendment
Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-
bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Parks: E-bikes and Electric Kick Scooters Pilot Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby has a new pilot program that allows e-bikes and electric kick scooters in some parks. This change amends the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby website for designated e-bike and scooter areas.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026

[image]
1.14812. Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amend Bylaw
No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979
(e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby: E-bike, Scooter Bylaw Enforcement Changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby changed its bylaw about how it enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. The city wants to manage these programs better. This update affects how the city handles violations of the rules for e-bikes and scooters.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated rules for e-bikes and scooters.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick
scooter programs)
[image]
1.14813 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 3, 2026 BBY Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 .pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Property Tax Rates Set for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby has passed Bylaw #14814, also called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This bylaw sets the property tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby for 2026. The bylaw is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (provincial law that gives municipalities certain powers).
briefs.action_required: Pay your 2026 property taxes to the City of Burnaby on time.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
[image]
1.14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and
improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026)

Subject to approval of item 7.3.
